items.verse: فَدَلَّىٰهُمَا بِغُرُورٍۢ ۚ فَلَمَّا ذَاقَا ٱلشَّجَرَةَ بَدَتْ لَهُمَا سَوْءَٰتُهُمَا وَطَفِقَا يَخْصِفَانِ عَلَيْهِمَا مِن وَرَقِ ٱلْجَنَّةِ ۖ وَنَادَىٰهُمَا رَبُّهُمَآ أَلَمْ أَنْهَكُمَا عَن تِلْكُمَا ٱلشَّجَرَةِ وَأَقُل لَّكُمَآ إِنَّ ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنَ لَكُمَا عَدُوٌّۭ مُّبِينٌۭ
items.verseEn: So he made them fall, through deception. And when they tasted of the tree, their private parts became apparent to them, and they began to fasten together over themselves from the leaves of Paradise. And their Lord called to them, "Did I not forbid you from that tree and tell you that Satan is to you a clear enemy?"

items.verse: فَأَجَآءَهَا ٱلْمَخَاضُ إِلَىٰ جِذْعِ ٱلنَّخْلَةِ قَالَتْ يَـٰلَيْتَنِى مِتُّ قَبْلَ هَـٰذَا وَكُنتُ نَسْيًۭا مَّنسِيًّۭا
items.verseEn: And the pains of childbirth drove her to the trunk of a palm tree. She said, "Oh, I wish I had died before this and was in oblivion, forgotten."

items.verse: وَهُوَ ٱلَّذِىٓ أَنزَلَ مِنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ مَآءًۭ فَأَخْرَجْنَا بِهِۦ نَبَاتَ كُلِّ شَىْءٍۢ فَأَخْرَجْنَا مِنْهُ خَضِرًۭا نُّخْرِجُ مِنْهُ حَبًّۭا مُّتَرَاكِبًۭا وَمِنَ ٱلنَّخْلِ مِن طَلْعِهَا قِنْوَانٌۭ دَانِيَةٌۭ وَجَنَّـٰتٍۢ مِّنْ أَعْنَابٍۢ وَٱلزَّيْتُونَ وَٱلرُّمَّانَ مُشْتَبِهًۭا وَغَيْرَ مُتَشَـٰبِهٍ ۗ ٱنظُرُوٓا۟ إِلَىٰ ثَمَرِهِۦٓ إِذَآ أَثْمَرَ وَيَنْعِهِۦٓ ۚ إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكُمْ لَـَٔايَـٰتٍۢ لِّقَوْمٍۢ يُؤْمِنُونَ
items.verseEn: And it is He who sends down rain from the sky, and We produce thereby the growth of all things. We produce from it greenery from which We produce grains arranged in layers. And from the palm trees - of its emerging fruit are clusters hanging low. And [We produce] gardens of grapevines and olives and pomegranates, similar yet varied. Look at [each of] its fruit when it yields and [at] its ripening. Indeed in that are signs for a people who believe.

items.verse: إِنَّهَا شَجَرَةٌۭ تَخْرُجُ فِىٓ أَصْلِ ٱلْجَحِيمِ
items.verseEn: Indeed, it is a tree issuing from the bottom of the Hellfire,

items.verse: وَقَالَ فِرْعَوْنُ يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلْمَلَأُ مَا عَلِمْتُ لَكُم مِّنْ إِلَـٰهٍ غَيْرِى فَأَوْقِدْ لِى يَـٰهَـٰمَـٰنُ عَلَى ٱلطِّينِ فَٱجْعَل لِّى صَرْحًۭا لَّعَلِّىٓ أَطَّلِعُ إِلَىٰٓ إِلَـٰهِ مُوسَىٰ وَإِنِّى لَأَظُنُّهُۥ مِنَ ٱلْكَـٰذِبِينَ
items.verseEn: And Pharaoh said, "O eminent ones, I have not known you to have a god other than me. Then ignite for me, O Hāmān, [a fire] upon the clay and make for me a tower that I may look at the God of Moses. And indeed, I do think he is among the liars."

items.verse: ۞ وَلَقَدْ ءَاتَيْنَا دَاوُۥدَ مِنَّا فَضْلًۭا ۖ يَـٰجِبَالُ أَوِّبِى مَعَهُۥ وَٱلطَّيْرَ ۖ وَأَلَنَّا لَهُ ٱلْحَدِيدَ
items.verseEn: And We certainly gave David from Us bounty. [We said], "O mountains, repeat [Our] praises with him, and the birds [as well]." And We made pliable for him iron,

items.verse: ءَاتُونِى زُبَرَ ٱلْحَدِيدِ ۖ حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا سَاوَىٰ بَيْنَ ٱلصَّدَفَيْنِ قَالَ ٱنفُخُوا۟ ۖ حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا جَعَلَهُۥ نَارًۭا قَالَ ءَاتُونِىٓ أُفْرِغْ عَلَيْهِ قِطْرًۭا
items.verseEn: Bring me bars of iron" - until, when he had leveled [them] between the two mountain walls, he said, "Blow [with bellows]," until when he had made it [like] fire, he said, "Bring me, that I may pour over it molten copper."
And [with] Thamūd, who carved out the rocks in the valley?

items.verse: فَوَسْوَسَ إِلَيْهِ ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنُ قَالَ يَـٰٓـَٔادَمُ هَلْ أَدُلُّكَ عَلَىٰ شَجَرَةِ ٱلْخُلْدِ وَمُلْكٍۢ لَّا يَبْلَىٰ
items.verseEn: Then Satan whispered to him; he said, "O Adam, shall I direct you to the tree of eternity and possession that will not deteriorate?"

items.verse: ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنُ يَعِدُكُمُ ٱلْفَقْرَ وَيَأْمُرُكُم بِٱلْفَحْشَآءِ ۖ وَٱللَّهُ يَعِدُكُم مَّغْفِرَةًۭ مِّنْهُ وَفَضْلًۭا ۗ وَٱللَّهُ وَٰسِعٌ عَلِيمٌۭ
items.verseEn: Satan threatens you with poverty and orders you to immorality, while Allāh promises you forgiveness from Him and bounty. And Allāh is all-Encompassing and Knowing.

items.verse: ٱلَّذِينَ يَأْكُلُونَ ٱلرِّبَوٰا۟ لَا يَقُومُونَ إِلَّا كَمَا يَقُومُ ٱلَّذِى يَتَخَبَّطُهُ ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنُ مِنَ ٱلْمَسِّ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ بِأَنَّهُمْ قَالُوٓا۟ إِنَّمَا ٱلْبَيْعُ مِثْلُ ٱلرِّبَوٰا۟ ۗ وَأَحَلَّ ٱللَّهُ ٱلْبَيْعَ وَحَرَّمَ ٱلرِّبَوٰا۟ ۚ فَمَن جَآءَهُۥ مَوْعِظَةٌۭ مِّن رَّبِّهِۦ فَٱنتَهَىٰ فَلَهُۥ مَا سَلَفَ وَأَمْرُهُۥٓ إِلَى ٱللَّهِ ۖ وَمَنْ عَادَ فَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ أَصْحَـٰبُ ٱلنَّارِ ۖ هُمْ فِيهَا خَـٰلِدُونَ
items.verseEn: Those who consume interest cannot stand [on the Day of Resurrection] except as one stands who is being beaten by Satan into insanity. That is because they say, "Trade is [just] like interest." But Allāh has permitted trade and has forbidden interest. So whoever has received an admonition from his Lord and desists may have what is past, and his affair rests with Allāh. But whoever returns [to dealing in interest or usury] - those are the companions of the Fire; they will abide eternally therein.

items.verse: وَقُل لِّعِبَادِى يَقُولُوا۟ ٱلَّتِى هِىَ أَحْسَنُ ۚ إِنَّ ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنَ يَنزَغُ بَيْنَهُمْ ۚ إِنَّ ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنَ كَانَ لِلْإِنسَـٰنِ عَدُوًّۭا مُّبِينًۭا
items.verseEn: And tell My servants to say that which is best. Indeed, Satan induces [dissension] among them. Indeed Satan is ever, to mankind, a clear enemy.

items.verse: قَالَ بَصُرْتُ بِمَا لَمْ يَبْصُرُوا۟ بِهِۦ فَقَبَضْتُ قَبْضَةًۭ مِّنْ أَثَرِ ٱلرَّسُولِ فَنَبَذْتُهَا وَكَذَٰلِكَ سَوَّلَتْ لِى نَفْسِى
items.verseEn: He said, "I saw what they did not see, so I took a handful [of dust] from the track of the messenger and threw it, and thus did my soul entice me."

items.verse: ٱللَّهُ يَبْسُطُ ٱلرِّزْقَ لِمَن يَشَآءُ وَيَقْدِرُ ۚ وَفَرِحُوا۟ بِٱلْحَيَوٰةِ ٱلدُّنْيَا وَمَا ٱلْحَيَوٰةُ ٱلدُّنْيَا فِى ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ إِلَّا مَتَـٰعٌۭ
items.verseEn: Allāh extends provision for whom He wills and restricts [it]. And they rejoice in the worldly life, while the worldly life is not, compared to the Hereafter, except [brief] enjoyment.

items.verse: فَأَلْقَىٰ مُوسَىٰ عَصَاهُ فَإِذَا هِىَ تَلْقَفُ مَا يَأْفِكُونَ
items.verseEn: Then Moses threw his staff, and at once it devoured what they falsified.

items.verse: يُقَلِّبُ ٱللَّهُ ٱلَّيْلَ وَٱلنَّهَارَ ۚ إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَعِبْرَةًۭ لِّأُو۟لِى ٱلْأَبْصَـٰرِ
items.verseEn: Allāh alternates the night and the day. Indeed in that is a lesson for those who have vision.

harf.hint: A colorless blessing from the sky, essential for purification.
harf.arabicVerse: إِذْ يُغَشِّيكُمُ ٱلنُّعَاسَ أَمَنَةًۭ مِّنْهُ وَيُنَزِّلُ عَلَيْكُم مِّنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ مَآءًۭ لِّيُطَهِّرَكُم بِهِۦ وَيُذْهِبَ عَنكُمْ رِجْزَ ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنِ وَلِيَرْبِطَ عَلَىٰ قُلُوبِكُمْ وَيُثَبِّتَ بِهِ ٱلْأَقْدَامَ
harf.verse: 8:11 — [Remember] when He overwhelmed you with drowsiness [giving] security from Him and sent down upon you from the sky, rain by which to purify you and remove from you the evil [suggestions] of Satan and to make steadfast your hearts and plant firmly thereby your feet.

deduction.title: The Voice of Concealed Conviction
deduction.intro: In the heart of a tyrannical court, where disbelief was the law of the land, a lone soul walked a tightrope of faith and fear. This individual, close to the center of power, listened as a prophet's message was met with scorn and threats. A moment of truth arrived, forcing this person to choose between silent safety and speaking a dangerous truth. Can you uncover the identity of this courageous advocate?
clues: I lived within the very household of a tyrant, witnessing firsthand the arrogance that rejected divine signs.
I kept my faith a secret, a hidden treasure in a palace of idolatry.
I heard the leader I served plot to kill a messenger of Allah, a man named Musa.
I questioned their logic, reminding them of the fate of the people of Nuh, 'Ad, and Thamud.
I posed a critical question to my people: 'If he is a liar, his lie is upon him; but if he is truthful, some of what he promises will strike you.'
My story is told as an example of faith in the surah named 'The Forgiver,' where I warned my people against the schemes of the powerful.

identity.label: Who Am I?
options: The Believer from Fir'awn's Family
Haman
The Magicians of Fir'awn
Qarun
The Samiri

trial.label: What Was My Trial?
options: Defending Prophet Musa in the tyrant's court
Building a lofty tower to reach the heavens
Being cast into a blazing fire
Being swallowed by a giant fish
Losing all wealth and health
trial.answer: Defending Prophet Musa in the tyrant's court

location.label: What Was My Argument's Theme?
options: The consequence of rejecting a prophet's message
The demand for a sign from a sacred tree
The creation of a calf from molten jewelry
The miracle of a camel from rock
The interpretation of a ruler's dreams
location.answer: The consequence of rejecting a prophet's message

outcome.label: What Was the Outcome?
options: Allah saved this individual from their evil plot
The entire court immediately accepted Islam
The tyrant repented at the moment of drowning
A great flood destroyed the disbelievers
The individual was exiled to a distant land
outcome.answer: Allah saved this individual from their evil plot

deduction.arabic: فَوَقَىٰهُ ٱللَّهُ سَيِّـَٔاتِ مَا مَكَرُوا۟ ۖ وَحَاقَ بِـَٔالِ فِرْعَوْنَ سُوٓءُ ٱلْعَذَابِ
deduction.verse: So Allāh protected him from the evils they plotted, and the people of Pharaoh were enveloped by the worst of punishment - (40:45)

translations: And not We have sent you
scramble.hint: This verse describes the universal purpose of the Prophet Muhammad's (ﷺ) mission to all of creation.
scramble.verseEn: And We have not sent you, [O Muḥammad], except as a mercy to the worlds.

verse.arabic_text: وَمَا ٱلْحَيَوٰةُ ٱلدُّنْيَآ إِلَّا لَعِبٌ وَلَهْوٌ ۖ وَلَلدَّارُ ٱلْءَاخِرَةُ خَيْرٌ لِّلَّذِينَ يَتَّقُونَ ۗ أَفَلَا تَعْقِلُونَ
verse.translation: And the worldly life is not but amusement and diversion; but the home of the Hereafter is best for those who fear Allah, so will you not reason?

theme_question.correct: The true value and permanence of the Hereafter compared to the fleeting nature of worldly life.
options: The obligation to earn a living and enjoy the lawful pleasures of the world.
The true value and permanence of the Hereafter compared to the fleeting nature of worldly life.
The prohibition of specific worldly amusements like music and games.
A command to completely abandon the world and live in seclusion.

educational_notes.verse_context: This verse is part of a larger discourse addressing the disbelievers of Makkah who were engrossed in their worldly life and rejected the Prophet's (ﷺ) message about the Hereafter. It serves as a powerful rhetorical question, urging them to use their intellect to understand the true reality of existence.
educational_notes.theme_explanation: The theme is a fundamental Quranic principle: the hierarchy of values between the dunya (this world) and the akhirah (the Hereafter). The verse uses the metaphor of 'amusement and diversion' (لَعِبٌ وَلَهْوٌ) to describe the world's deceptive and temporary nature. True, lasting success and goodness are reserved for those who have Taqwa (God-consciousness, piety) and prioritize the life to come. The final question, 'so will you not reason?' (أَفَلَا تَعْقِلُونَ), challenges the listener to reflect deeply on their priorities.
educational_notes.surah_overview: Surah Al-An'am ('The Cattle') is the 6th chapter of the Quran and is unique as it was revealed entirely in Makkah in one sitting. Its central theme is Tawhid (the absolute Oneness of Allah) and the refutation of shirk (polytheism). It addresses core tenets of faith, including Allah's sovereignty, creation, prophethood, and the reality of resurrection and judgment, often using the signs in the natural world as proof of the Creator's power and wisdom. The surah's name comes from the discussion of various pagan superstitions related to cattle (ayahs 136-140).
